_note: This is the only file you need to maintain. Each foundry is one object. New foundry = new object with its own 'slug'. Order doesn't matter — the table sorts alphabetically.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: Total headcount of the client determines the price — not the studio size, not the number of users.

rows.t: Total headcount of the client ("Company Size") — not the studio size, not the number of users.
rows.sub: A single metric for all license types. Web traffic or app downloads don't need to be estimated.

rows.t: The client ("License Owner"), not the designer. The designing studio is covered under the client's license.
rows.sub: Each client needs their own license, even when the same studio does the work repeatedly.

rows.t: One-time payment, no subscription. An upgrade is required if the client grows beyond the licensed company size.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: Desktop, Web, App, Video, and Social Media are separate licenses — one does not cover another.

rows.t: Installation on all devices; print, digital documents, objects, merchandise, signage, portfolio.
rows.sub: Under 50 employees: logo license included. Under 3 employees: Social Media and Video licenses also included.

rows.t: WOFF2 on one domain via @font-face, including email newsletters. Additional domain = additional license. Self-hosting and subsetting permitted.
Embedding in one app (game, desktop, mobile, web app, POS). Platform variants included. Multiple apps = multiple licenses.
Motion content for one brand — YouTube, Netflix, TV, advertising, video billboards. Social media video requires an additional Social Media license.
Design assets for one brand on social channels. Separate license, distinct from Web.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: No hidden surcharges for the most common use cases — logo use is the frequent exception.

rows.t: One logo license per brand. Included free when the client has fewer than 50 employees.
rows.sub: The font may be converted to outlines and modified for logo creation.

rows.t: Signage is part of the Desktop / Print license.
Required so that third parties (follow-on designers, agencies, developers) may use the font on behalf of the client.
Only permitted for logos (outlines). Otherwise no reverse engineering, no derivative fonts, no format conversion.
Fonts may not be used to train AI models.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: Free trial fonts with the full character set, plus discounts for students, non-profits, and teaching staff.

rows.t: Free, full character set. For purchase decisions and pitching concepts to clients.
rows.sub: Requested via a form on the Dinamo website.

rows.t: May also be used for commercial personal projects. After graduation, continues to be valid for ongoing personal projects. Client work requires a client license.
rows.sub: Student Font Pack: educational license covering many typefaces across all media types.

rows.t: For registered non-profits and cultural institutions (museums, galleries, artists, independent publishers and labels, etc.).
Educational discount for non-commercial teaching materials at accredited institutions.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: German law, jurisdiction Berlin. License is non-transferable.

rows.t: Bundled license covering all media types, with no limit on company size or subcontractors. Required for publicly listed companies or those with annual revenue exceeding €100 million.
License is non-transferable and non-assignable.
German law, jurisdiction Berlin. EULA version 2.49.
No political use without permission; no violent or discriminatory content; not on public servers or in public repositories.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: Desktop is billed per user and location — web is separate, measured by pageviews.

rows.t: Desktop licenses are calculated based on the number of users (employees who install or have access to the font) and the number of locations.
rows.sub: Classic model: anyone working with the font needs coverage. Web is measured separately by pageviews.

rows.t: The licensing company. Agencies, partners, and freelancers working on behalf of a client need their own 3rd-party desktop license.
rows.sub: Each commissioned party purchases separately, directly from Lineto.

rows.t: One-time payment, no subscription.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: Desktop, Web, App, and Campaign are separate licenses — some only available as extensions.

rows.t: Fonts on workstations for creating print and digital assets. Includes basic rights for printed media such as books, periodicals, flyers, brochures.
rows.sub: For corporate and campaign print, an additional license may be required.

rows.t: Webfonts for dynamic text on specified domains, via @font-face. WOFF and WOFF2, self-hosting. One web license includes one development domain.
License extension for native, web, or hybrid apps on stationary, mobile, or embedded devices.
Dedicated licensing tier for larger organisations.
Separate license for extensive print campaigns beyond the base desktop license.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Lineto is known for restrictive terms — logo, social, video, and merchandise require separate licensing.

rows.t: Logo, social media, video, and merchandise use each require a separate license beyond the desktop license.
rows.sub: Before any brand use, it is advisable to contact Lineto directly.

rows.t: A 3rd-party desktop license extension is required for agencies, partners, and freelancers working on behalf of an existing licensee.
rows.sub: Each commissioned party purchases their own license directly from Lineto.

rows.t: Editing the font files is not permitted.
Special conditions apply for use by political or religious organisations.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: No freely downloadable trial fonts — but a 75% student discount.

rows.t: Lineto does not provide freely downloadable trial fonts in the same way as other foundries; testing happens through the standard purchase channels.
75% discount on the retail price with proof of enrolment. For print and web work in educational contexts; commercial use requires the client to hold a full license.
rows.sub: Valid for the duration of studies; afterwards a 3-month window for an upgrade to a full license at a reduced rate.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: Swiss law. License is non-transferable.

rows.t: Dedicated licensing tier for the requirements of larger organisations.
The license is non-transferable.
Swiss law. Lineto may amend the EULA terms at any time; changes are published on the website.
The licensee must inform all persons with access to the font of the license terms and ensure compliance.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: Desktop per computer, Web by unique visitors, App by developer count — three transparent metrics.

rows.t: Three main types with their own metric: Desktop per computer within an organisation, Web by unique monthly visitors, App by number of developers.
rows.sub: Modular and transparent — each type is offered in multiple tiers.

rows.t: The purchasing organisation. Licenses are not cross-organisational: anyone producing their own output needs their own license; the client does too.
rows.sub: When purchasing for a third party you remain bound by the EULA but may not use the font yourself.

rows.t: One-time payment, no subscription. Upgrades are charged only as the difference to the higher tier.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: Desktop, Web, and App are separate licenses — Desktop also covers broadcast and OOH.

rows.t: Use in applications producing output such as Adobe CC, Figma, MS Office — print, video, social media posts, logos. PDF embedding permitted; embedding in other formats is not. Per computer.
rows.sub: Also covers broadcast, advertising, and OOH as long as the producing computers are desktop-licensed.

rows.t: Webfonts via @font-face, any number of domains and servers within the organisation. WOFF / WOFF2, self-hosting only. Measured by unique monthly visitors.
Embedding in desktop and mobile apps. Measured by number of developers — no limit on app count, platform, or downloads.
Automated document generation and tools such as Canva. Measured by documents per year. Available on request.
Ads with embedded font files for live text. Measured by impressions per year.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Logo use is included in Desktop at no extra charge — no special license required.

rows.t: No special license needed — logos are covered by the Desktop license as long as the producing computers are licensed.
Own license to embed fonts in MS Office files shared with third parties. Measured by computer count.
Own license to ship a product with an embedded font (e.g. set-top box).
Licenses are per organisation. When purchasing for a client, the client needs their own license for their own output.
Font files may not be altered. Converting to outlines in a design tool is permitted — standard practice for logos.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: All typefaces available as trials — restricted character set, for mockups and prototyping.

rows.t: All typefaces available as trials — minimal character set, no OpenType features. Permitted for mockups and prototypes, including for client presentations.
rows.sub: Students may use trials for academic, non-commercial projects without a further license.

rows.t: Grilli Junior: discount for students and recent graduates, applied at checkout.
Multi-license and family discounts available directly in the shop.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: Two EULAs depending on the licensee's location (US vs. rest of world), both under Swiss law.

rows.t: Dedicated licensing tier for larger organisations; Unlimited tier from $10,000 per style.
Licenses are not cross-organisational and may not be shared with clients.

Two separate EULAs depending on the licensee's location — one for the US, one for the rest of the world. Both are governed by Swiss law; the place of jurisdiction is Lucerne (rest of world) or Brooklyn, NY (US).
A breach terminates the license and incurs a penalty of $4,000 or, if higher, 50% of the license fee.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: One all-inclusive license based on company size — Web, App, and Broadcast are included at no extra charge.

rows.t: The price is based on the total headcount of the company. The entry price covers up to 25 employees.
rows.sub: A single all-inclusive license instead of separate purchases per media type.

rows.t: The licensing company. Both the end user and any party working on their behalf must be covered by a valid license or the Sharing Extension.
One-time purchase, no annual fee, no renewal — perpetual.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: The Standard license covers almost everything — print, web, app, social, broadcast, video in one.

rows.t: One Standard license covers almost all uses: print, websites, mobile apps, social media, e-publications, broadcasting, videos, and films.
rows.sub: Desktop and webfonts are included with every purchase at no extra charge.

rows.t: Included in the Standard license — no separate purchase, no pageview limit.
Included in the Standard license — mobile apps are covered.
Broadcasting, videos, and films are part of the Standard license.
Installation on all devices, online and offline; storage on collaboration tools, servers, and cloud services is permitted.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Virtually no special cases — logo is included in the Standard; the optional Sharing Extension covers third parties.

rows.t: The Standard license covers almost all use cases — no separate logo license as required by other foundries.
Optional extension to the Company license so that third parties and contractors (e.g. agencies) are automatically covered.
rows.sub: Without the Sharing Extension, every third party needs their own license.

rows.t: For corporate groups, a separate license covering affiliated companies is available on request.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: Pioneer in free trials since 2007 — and a lifetime Personal license for students.

rows.t: Swiss Typefaces was the first foundry to offer free trials, since 2007. All typefaces available as Desktop and Webfont trials, legally usable in your own designs.
rows.sub: Trials in reduced scope: full Latin upper- and lowercase with basic punctuation; full scope on request.

rows.t: Special pricing on all typefaces, including a lifetime Personal license — valid during and after studies.
rows.sub: The license is in the student's name and is non-transferable.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: Swiss law. All scripts and features in one file, one price.

rows.t: The license is based on headcount; a Sharing Extension extends it to third parties.
Licenses — including the student Personal license — are non-transferable.
All typefaces include every available writing system and feature in a single file, at one price.
Swiss law.

foundries.name: Colophon
foundries.status: Joined Monotype in 2023; no longer sold directly since March 2025 — now via MyFonts and Monotype Fonts.

blocks.title: Status & Distribution
blocks.lead: No longer sold directly since March 2025 — the library is available through MyFonts and Monotype Fonts.

rows.t: Since March 2025, Colophon no longer sells through its own website. The library is part of Monotype Fonts and available via MyFonts.
rows.sub: License terms, pricing, and availability are now governed by the respective distributor.

rows.t: Desktop licenses are measured by number of users — the number of computers on which the font is installed. Cumulatively expandable.
Depends on the distributor model — at MyFonts, webfonts are frequently offered as annual subscriptions.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: Desktop, Web, App, and ePub are separate licenses — all available via MyFonts or Monotype.

rows.t: A Desktop license typically covers 1–3 users at one location: print, logo creation, working on-screen and in DTP software. PDF embedding is included.
rows.sub: More users and multiple locations are possible; does not cover web or app use.

rows.t: Self-hosted webfonts, per individual website. Tiered by monthly pageviews. Agencies may not share a web license across multiple websites.
Separate license for iOS and Android apps, distinct from Desktop and Web. One-time fee, tiered by app price and estimated downloads.
Embedding in electronic publications, measured by number of publications — each issue counts separately.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Logo use is part of the Desktop license — but check the EULA per typeface as terms may vary.

rows.t: Logo creation is part of the Desktop license; a logo on a website is covered by the Desktop license as it is treated as an image.
Included in the Desktop license — but does not apply to embedded web or app use.
At MyFonts, restrictions may apply depending on the typeface — regarding logos / trademarks, number of locations, or products intended for sale. Check the EULA for each case.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: Trial fonts available for testing — student discounts: check with the distributor.

rows.t: When requesting trials, a trial license applies: permits testing the font across various media, but must be upgraded before productive use.
Terms have been subject to the distributor since the Monotype acquisition — check directly with MyFonts or Monotype.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: Brand remains with Colophon Foundry — terms are set by the respective distributor.

rows.t: The Colophon typefaces are trademarks of the AS & EH Partnership Limited, trading as Colophon Foundry.
Individual typefaces via MyFonts; the full library as part of a Monotype Fonts subscription.
Subject to the terms and conditions of the respective distributor.
Depends on the distributor and license route; the specific EULA at the time of purchase is the authoritative document.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: Desktop is counted per computer, web by unique monthly visitors per domain — expandable from your Optimo account.

rows.t: Desktop is licensed per computing device ("Licensed Unit") in the locations named in the order; web is measured per domain by the number of unique monthly visitors.
rows.sub: Add users to a Desktop License or raise the web-traffic tier later from the Orders section of your Optimo account.

rows.t: Every entity working with the font needs its own license. If an agency hires a freelancer or external contractor for a project using an Optimo font, each party must buy a license.
One-time purchase; expand users or web traffic later via an upgrade. All sales are final.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: Optimo unbundles almost every use into its own license — Desktop and Web are only the start.

rows.t: Install and use on the number of computing devices in the specified locations, for creating print and static digital documents.
@font-face on one or more domains, tiered by unique monthly visitors. Upgrade the license once the threshold is exceeded.
Separate license to embed a font in a native mobile application (Android, iOS, Windows Mobile) to style and display text.
Separate license for embedding fonts in e-books, e-magazines, and e-newspapers.
Social Media, Merchandise, Trademark, Streaming/TV/Cinema, Digital Ads, Campaign, and Server are further separate licenses.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Nearly every distinct use — social, merchandise, video, ads — needs its own license on top of Desktop.

rows.t: Displaying the font on social platforms (Facebook, Instagram, etc.), for organic posts or paid ads, needs a Social Media License.
Displaying the font on physical products (t-shirts, promotional items, packaging, bottles) needs a Merchandise License.
Streaming, TV, cinema, video ads, digital ad banners, and time-limited campaigns each have their own license (Streaming/TV/Cinema, Digital Ads, Campaign).
Registering a logo that uses an Optimo font as a trademark needs a Trademark License; a Desktop License must be secured first.
Modifying the font (including via AI) is prohibited. Prior use without a license is charged retroactively plus a surcharge of at least 200%.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: Free trials for testing and pitching, and an 80% student discount — but no negotiated discounts otherwise.

rows.t: Trial License with a limited character set (OTF), strictly for testing, evaluation, and pitching in an internal, non-commercial context.
80% off the standard price; install on a personal computer and use until the end of studies. Educational institutions can request a custom Educational License.
Optimo does not grant discounts to individuals or organisations; instead it offers flat, equitable pricing with lower prices for families, packages, and added styles.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: Swiss law, jurisdiction Geneva. License is non-transferable; copyright stays with Optimo.

rows.t: Custom and bespoke licensing is available on request via quote.optimo.ch.
The license cannot be assigned, transferred, or otherwise disposed of, in whole or in part, without Optimo's prior written consent.
Buying a license grants usage rights only — copyright in the font remains with Optimo Sàrl.
Swiss substantive law; the exclusive place of jurisdiction is the courts of the Canton of Geneva.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: Desktop is counted per computer; the media scope is set by choosing a Print or an All Uses license.

rows.t: Each Desktop license covers one computer — ten workstations means ten licenses. The permitted media depends on the chosen license: Print only, or All Uses.
rows.sub: All variable fonts are also available as free trials with a reduced character set.

rows.t: Each entity needs its own licenses. A client's project is not covered by the designer's licenses — the client needs their own.
One-time payment; the student license is a lifetime license including future updates.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: Two scopes: Print for print-only work, All Uses for print plus all digital media.

rows.t: For print-based projects only — brochures, books, packaging, posters. Does not include digital use such as websites or apps.
Covers print and all digital applications: websites, social media, apps, and broadcast. No separate web-traffic tier.
All variable fonts available for free trials with a reduced character set (full Latin upper- and lowercase, basic punctuation).

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Digital, app, and broadcast use are bundled into the All Uses license rather than sold separately.

rows.t: Not separate licenses — all covered by the All Uses license, with no page-view or download tiers.
Not addressed on the licensing page — check the specific EULA before relying on logo or trademark use.
Licenses are per entity; a client needs their own licenses for their own use.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: Free variable-font trials, and an unusually generous lifetime student license.

rows.t: Free trials of all variable fonts with a reduced character set, for evaluation and testing.
A font at CHF 50 as a lifetime license — usable throughout studies and into professional work, including all future updates.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: A Swiss studio based in Lausanne — see the individual EULA for the full legal terms.

rows.t: NewGlyph is a Swiss type studio (newglyph SA) based in Lausanne, founded in 2019 by Ian Party after leaving Swiss Typefaces.
Swiss company domiciled in Lausanne. The specific EULA for each license is the authoritative document for legal terms.
Not stated on the licensing page — refer to the EULA.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: Price is built at checkout from the context of use — media type, number of users, and client size.

rows.t: Licenses are constructed around parameters informed by the context of use: media type (print, web, etc.), number of users, and the size of the client. These are entered at checkout to build an accurate license and fee.
rows.sub: Desktop is priced by number of users; web by page views.

rows.t: When a designer works on a project for a client, the client is the license holder. Designers and freelancers are covered under the client's license and do not need a separate one.
A one-time cost for the right to use the typeface in the situations covered by the contract.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: Desktop and Web are the standard licenses; several uses are handled on request.

rows.t: Priced by the number of users, for creating print and static assets.
Webfonts priced by page views, per website.
ePublishing, brand mark / logotype, merchandising, streaming / TV / cinema, server storage, advertising campaigns, and non-profit use are licensed by contacting Source Type directly.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Logo and brand-mark use, and most non-standard media, require a direct request.

rows.t: Brand-mark and logotype licensing is not part of the standard checkout — contact Source Type directly for a quote.
Modifying the typeface is not permitted; for customisations, Source Type can provide a quote.
Because the client holds the license, designers working on the client's behalf are covered without a separate license.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: Free trial fonts, plus a 50% student package for non-commercial work.

rows.t: Trial fonts with a limited character set (A–Z, a–z, 0–9) are free to download from each typeface page, for evaluation and student work.
A Student License Package gives 50% off, selectable at checkout. For non-commercial work only; commercial projects need a full license held by the client.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: A Zurich-based studio; licenses are non-transferable.

rows.t: Source Type GmbH, Zweierstrasse 100, 8003 Zurich, Switzerland.
Licenses cannot be transferred or used for purposes other than those initially specified.
Swiss company domiciled in Zurich; the specific EULA at purchase governs the detailed legal terms.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: Desktop and Web are usually bought together as one combined license, discounted as a combo.

rows.t: Desktop is priced by the number of font users; web by the number of domains and current web traffic. A combined Desktop + Web license is offered at a 75% combo discount.
rows.sub: One license covers one legal entity for the mediums and numeric limits set at purchase.

rows.t: Every font user must be covered by a license. Each license is valid for one legal entity only; separate entities buy separate licenses. Fonts may not be shared with clients — a client needs their own license.
Upon full payment, a perpetual, non-exclusive, non-transferable license.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: A broad Desktop + Web license plus a separate Mobile App license.

rows.t: Covers desktop use, print, packaging, video, logotypes, and social media under 100k followers, plus self-hosted webfonts on the licensed domain(s).
Separate license to embed fonts in apps, priced by app count and monthly users.
Any use beyond the covered mediums is forbidden and needs additional licensing.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Logotypes are included, but trademark registration and large advertising are not.

rows.t: Logotypes are covered by the Desktop + Web license. Registering the font as a trademark is excluded and needs separate licensing.
Advertising campaigns and (D)OOH for commercial companies are excluded from the base license.
Fonts cannot be shared with clients or third parties; if a client requires the fonts, a license must be purchased for the client.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: Free trial fonts are offered; a Desktop + Web combo carries a 75% discount.

rows.t: Free trial fonts are available from the Fatype website for evaluation.
Buying Desktop and Web together applies a 75% combo discount versus separate licenses.
No student or educational discount is stated on the licensing pages.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: Swiss law, jurisdiction Neuchâtel. License is non-transferable.

rows.t: Fatype Sàrl, a Swiss limited company.
The license is non-transferable.
Governed by the laws of Switzerland; disputes are resolved in the courts of Neuchâtel, Switzerland.

blocks.title: Pricing Model
blocks.lead: Desktop is counted by users (not computers); web and app each have their own metric, chosen at purchase.

rows.t: Desktop uses the metric number of users, not installs — each user may install on as many devices as needed. The base tier is 1–5 users. Web and app each use their own metric.
rows.sub: The minimum web tier is 20,000 page views or 5,000 unique users; the app tier starts at 2,500 MAUs or downloads.

rows.t: Klim recommends that the client purchases the desktop font licence and becomes the licence holder. Agencies may buy on a client's behalf but should not resell or profit from the licence.
A one-off cost for ongoing use, worldwide, with no expiry and no recurring fees. All sales are final.

blocks.title: License Types
blocks.lead: Separate licences for Desktop, Web, App, and Advertising — each with its own EULA.

rows.t: Priced by number of users; each user installs on any number of devices. Logos and logotypes are among the authorized uses.
Choose either monthly page views or monthly unique users at purchase; the metric cannot be switched later.
Choose monthly active users (MAUs) or total downloads; minimum tier 2,500.
A separate advertising font licence exists for advertising use.

blocks.title: Additional Costs & Special Cases
blocks.lead: Logo use is included in Desktop; modification is not allowed under standard licences.

rows.t: Logos and logotypes are explicitly listed as authorized uses under the Desktop licence — no separate licence required.
Using font-manipulation tools or attempting to modify the fonts to create derivative or substitute versions is prohibited under standard licences.
The end client should hold the licence; agencies may purchase on the client's behalf but must not resell or profit from it.

blocks.title: Discounts & Trials
blocks.lead: Test fonts cover the whole catalogue — but there is no student or educational discount.

rows.t: Free test fonts include all retail fonts in the catalogue, for evaluation and for student use.
Klim does not offer student or educational discounts; students can use the test fonts for their work.

blocks.title: Legal & Practical Info
blocks.lead: New Zealand law, jurisdiction Wellington. License is transferable only with consent.

rows.t: Klim Type Foundry, based in Wellington, New Zealand; founded by Kris Sowersby.
The licence may be assigned only with Klim's prior written consent, and the transferee must agree to be bound by all the terms.
Governed by the laws of New Zealand, within the exclusive jurisdiction of the New Zealand courts; disputes are arbitrated in Wellington.
